tweets.text: Is private credit worth it? What do you really get that's so great it's worth a 4% expense ratio? Yes, there's a 9% yield but the total return is more like 4%, meh, trails multi-sector bond funds, bank loans, junk bonds (which you can get for near free nowadays). Good note today from @DavidCohne questioning this whole asset class (or at least the interval funds that offer it)

tweets.text: If the US jobless rate falls to 4.2% or below, it'll be in line or below the expected 4.2% CPI next week. That would be just 7th time since 1960 with inflation running close to or above the jobless rate: BofA's Michael Hartnett. Previous times this happened: '66, '73, '90, '00, '08, '21.

tweets.text: Strong payroll report. Overall up 172K. Private up 120K. Leisure and hospitality added an anomalous 70K, possibly related to hiring for the upcoming World Cup.

thread_tweets.text: Strong payroll report. Overall up 172K. Private up 120K. Leisure and hospitality added an anomalous 70K, possibly related to hiring for the upcoming World Cup.
thread_tweets.full_text: Strong payroll report. Overall up 172K. Private up 120K. Leisure and hospitality added an anomalous 70K, possibly related to hiring for the upcoming World Cup.

thread_tweets.text: One big blemish: Full-time employment down 79K while part-time employment up 266K in the household survey, consistent with a temporary hiring surge.
thread_tweets.full_text: One big blemish: Full-time employment down 79K while part-time employment up 266K in the household survey, consistent with a temporary hiring surge.

tweets.full_text:
Strong payroll report. Overall up 172K. Private up 120K. Leisure and hospitality added an anomalous 70K, possibly related to hiring for the upcoming World Cup.

One big blemish: Full-time employment down 79K while part-time employment up 266K in the household survey, consistent with a temporary hiring surge.
